title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. ballii
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. ballii, also known as the Ball's columbine, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and soothing skin irritations. Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. ballii can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies.
